parsed_review.summary: CP00-443 settlement-core P06 permission-slice descriptor pack passes independent read-only review with no P0/P1/P2 defects; descriptor-only, no-write/no-real-data, read-only-reviewer, and no-Hermes-runtime boundaries are all verified consistent across plan, manifest, source, contract, and validation script. Two non-blocking P3 informational notes recorded.

findings.title: Downstream review-evidence artifacts not yet present (expected at this stage)
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts lists claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json and command-evidence.json currently exist in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-443/. Per the hardened_review_sequence and the command-evidence.json notes, these are post-review-gated artifacts produced after this read-only Claude run (finding_adjudication and construction_inspection steps). Consequently the 'adjudication' and 'inspection' evidence legs named in the review focus cannot be independently verified at this point; they remain to be produced downstream.
findings.recommendation: No action required by this review. Treat as by-design: the human/final approver should confirm the downstream adjudication, construction-inspection, and final closeout validation are completed (and human final approval recorded) before the pack is treated as closed and before any enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: production_ready_candidate must not be read as runtime/production readiness
findings.details: createSettlementCoreCp443HermesEvidencePacket sets production_ready_candidate:true when coverage.valid && slice.valid. This reflects only descriptor coverage and slice-shape validity. Pack-level production_ready is false and runtime_ready is false (manifest.json), and the descriptor explicitly defers runtime opening to CP00-444 with human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening:true. The naming could be misread as production readiness.
findings.recommendation: No code change required. Ensure downstream tooling and the human approver interpret production_ready_candidate strictly as descriptor-validity, not runtime/production readiness; the production_ready gate must remain false until runtime is explicitly opened with human approval.
